Galveston county emergency manager reported damage to a manufactured home at 427 9th Street in San Leon. Photos shared showed siding peeled off with a corner wall blown out. Part of the metal roofing was also peeled off. The raised foundation also appeared to be bent and or warped from the strong winds.

Broadcast media reported damage to a restaurant in Conroe after a line of thunderstorms pushed through the area. Photos shared by the restaurant on social media show partial roof damage with sheet metal roofing peeled up. Wind speeds were estimated around 78 mph based on a Small Retail Building with a degree of damage of 2.

Microburst produced a measured wind gust of 95 mph at the Palacious Airport. Most damage was confined to large tree branches snapped and some minor roof damage in the area.
Widespread tree damage and minor damage to some metal buildings along Buffalo Road.

An EF-1 tornado with 100 mph winds touched down just behind some apartments northeast of Wharton and travelled east across CR 166, CR 133, and CR 129. The most extensive damage was along Everett and Holiness Lanes, just south of CR 166. Numerous homes sustained major roof damage and a number of trees had large branches snapped. As the tornado traveled east it continued producing mostly tree damage and some minor roof damage.

An EF-0 tornado with winds of 75 mph began just east of Burr and produced some minor tree damage along its path as it travelled west across CR 117. An older shed also had its roof blown off along CR 111.

Emergency manager reported that 7 homes west of Goodrich were damaged by wind and hail. Most of these homes were located along FM 2969. Windows were knocked out, with some roof damage present. One mobile home had damaged siding. Wind speeds were estimated at 65 mph.
Emergency manager passed along reports of large hail west of Goodrich. One person was injured after hail came through the victims bedroom window, striking them in the head.
Multiple roads in Edna, TX covered in floodwaters from ongoing heavy rain. A few vehicles reported as stranded.

Numerous photos and videos were shared on social media, showing a waterspout near Galveston island, around 45th street beach. Broadcast media reported that the waterspout moved on to the beach, blowing umbrellas and chairs. However, no injuries were reported.

EF-0 tornado touched down south of Anderson producing tree and structural damage starting from CR406 traveling along Holland Creek toward FM 2819, CR 222, FM 2562, FM 1486, and CR232B. One home had all windows broken and a French door sucked out of the back of the home. Another home suffered extensive roof damage. Metal roofing was found twisted around a tree nearby.

A member of the public shared photos showing damage along Lake Riata Ln. Debris were scatted across the area with downed trees and visible roof damage to homes.
A storm spotter reported a funnel cloud near Grant Road. Storm spotter then observed the funnel cloud reaching the ground as a weak tornado.
A storm chaser shared photos on social media showing damage along village Hills Dr in Colony Creek. The photos showed numerous trees down with one large tree uprooted. Roof damage could also be seen.
Broadcast media reported damage in a neighborhood near Colony Creek Drive at Bayou Bluff Drive. Trees had also fallen on cars in the area.
Broadcast media reported damage along Summer Grove Circle. Trees had fallen onto cars and homes in the area.

Damaged was reported at the Klein Fire Department Admin complex along Stuebner Airline Road. Photos showed debris scattered across the area with downed fences and scattered traffic cones. Siding was peeled off portions of the engine building. A metal shed was also lofted and crushed in the adjacent neighborhood.
